config.title: Weltzeituhr FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers about Berlin's World Time Clock on Alexanderplatz.

items.q: Where is the Weltzeituhr in Berlin?
items.a: The Weltzeituhr stands on **Alexanderplatz 1**, directly under the TV Tower and close to the U-Bahn, S-Bahn, tram and bus stops.
It was presented to the public on **30 September 1969**, just before the 20th anniversary of the GDR.
The clock was designed by **Erich John**, a product and industrial designer connected with the Berlin-Weissensee art school.

items.q: Why did East Berlin build a world clock?
items.a: Alexanderplatz was redesigned as a modern socialist showcase square. The clock made the square look international, technical and open to the world, even though GDR travel was heavily restricted.
A rotating hour ring moves around city names grouped by **24 time zones**, so visitors can compare Berlin time with places around the world.
Yes. The clock was restored after reunification and has been listed as a protected Berlin monument since **July 2015**.
